title: tephrosia capensis var. acutifolia
intro: Tephrosia capensis var. acutifolia, also known as Bushman's tea or Yellow Pea, is a plant species native to Southern Africa, characterized by its yellow flowers and a narrow, pointed leaf shape. The medicinal properties of Tephrosia capensis var. acutifolia lie in its ability to act as a natural diuretic, reduce blood pressure, and possess anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits associated with consuming this plant include the treatment of kidney stones, urinary tract infections, and high blood pressure. Traditional herbal preparations of Tephrosia capensis var. acutifolia include making tea from the leaves, which is often used by indigenous communities in Southern Africa for its medicinal properties.
